% Auto-generated by compare_dust_grid.py
% Resolution: 512^3
\begin{deluxetable*}{lcccccccrccccc}
\tablecaption{CosmicGrain simulation runs at $512^3$ resolution, incrementing in dust-physics complexity\label{tab:dust_sim_ladder_512}}
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{4pt}
\tablewidth{0pt}
\tablehead{
  \colhead{Sim} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{Dust\\form.}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{Dust\\drag}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{Dust\\growth}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{ISM\\clump.}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{Therm.\\sput.}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{SN\\shocks}} &
  \colhead{\shortstack{Astr-\\ation}} &
  \colhead{$M_\star/M_{\rm halo}$} &
  \colhead{$\langle a\rangle$\,(nm)} &
  \colhead{$f_{\rm surv}$} &
  \colhead{$D/G$} &
  \colhead{$D/Z$} &
  \colhead{Notes}
}
\startdata
  S0 & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Baseline (no dust)} \\
  S1 & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add stellar sources + drag} \\
  S2 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add growth (uniform $n$)} \\
  S3 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add ISM density clumping} \\
  S4 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add coagulation/shattering} \\
  S5 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add thermal sputtering} \\
  S6 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \xmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add SN shock destruction} \\
  S7 & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \cmark & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \ldots & \tiny{Add astration (full model)} \\
\enddata
\tablecomments{%
  Each simulation builds on the previous by activating one additional
  dust-physics process; all runs use the same $512^3$ resolution and identical initial conditions (50~Mpc box, halo 569,
  $M_{200}\approx2\times10^{12}\,h^{-1}\,M_\odot$).
  All simulations include metal-line cooling.
  S0 establishes baseline galaxy properties with no dust physics.
  S1 introduces stellar dust sources (SNe~II + AGB) and gas--dust drag.
  S2 adds grain growth via ISM accretion assuming uniform gas density.
  S3 includes subgrid density clumping ($C=1$--30) for unresolved
  molecular clouds. S4 adds coagulation and shattering.
  S5--S7 add destruction mechanisms sequentially:
  thermal sputtering (S5), SN shock destruction (S6), and astration (S7).
  $\langle a\rangle$ is the mass-weighted mean grain radius;
  $f_{\rm surv}$ is the fraction of ever-created dust particles
  still alive at $z=0$; $D/G$ and $D/Z$ are measured in the ISM
  ($n_{\rm H}>0.1\,{\rm cm}^{-3}$) within $1.5\,R_{200}$ at $z=0$.}
\end{deluxetable*}
